title: Coryphopteris musashiensis
intro: Coryphopteris musashiensis, also known as Japanese club fern, is a species of fern native to Japan and parts of East Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Coryphopteris musashiensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Coryphopteris musashiensis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
taxonomy: Coryphopteris musashiensis is a species of fern within the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the order Polypodiales. This plant is classified under the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Pteridophyta. The genus Coryphopteris is a group of ferns that are primarily found in Asia. Further classification details for Coryphopteris musashiensis are not extensively documented.
